Subclasses: ScriptEditor public class TextAreaFrame extends javax.swing.JFrame A TextAreaFrame provides a text area in a JFrame window for viewing and editing text. The JTextArea is available from textArea() and you can call textArea().setText() or

xtArea() and you can call textArea().setText() or textArea().getText() to set and get the text. See Also: Serialized Form Inner classes inherited from class javax.swing.JFrame javax.swing.JFrame.AccessibleJFrame Inner classes inherited from class ja

the text, call textArea().getText(). isModified public boolean isModified () Return true if the text has been modified since the last save or since being set to new text. clearModified public void clearModified () Sets the modified state to false.

learModified () Sets the modified state to false. You should call this after opening a new file and setting the text because the text does not need to be changed at this point. windowClosing public void windowClosing (java.awt.event.WindowEvent ev

void windowClosing (java.awt.event.WindowEvent evt) This is called when the TextAreaFrame receives a windowClosing message. The default method just calls setVisible(false), but you can override it, for example, to check whether it is okay to close

for example, to check whether it is okay to close. If this method does nothing, the window is not closed. focusGained public void focusGained (java.awt.event.FocusEvent evt) This is called upon the text area focus gained. The default method does
